questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: To provide AI research papers and technical white‑papers.
options.explanation: The article does not mention research papers; it focuses on learning Notion.

options.text: To offer learning paths, badges, and certifications that help users master Notion.
options.explanation: The article clearly states that Notion Academy provides curated learning paths, badges, and certifications.

options.text: To sell hardware devices for office productivity.
options.explanation: Hardware sales are not part of Notion Academy’s offering.

options.text: To host gaming tournaments for the Notion community.
options.explanation: Gaming tournaments are not mentioned; the focus is on education.

questions.question: According to the article, what can you earn by completing a Notion Academy learning path?

options.text: A Notion Certified Admin credential.
options.explanation: Certification is a separate program; the article links it to a separate ‘Become a Notion Certified Admin’ section.

options.text: A Notion badge that shows you have completed the path.
options.explanation: The article explicitly says you can “Earn badges with Notion” by completing paths.

options.text: Free access to Notion’s premium plan for a year.
options.explanation: No mention of free premium access as a reward.

options.text: A monetary stipend for completing lessons.
options.explanation: The article does not discuss any financial compensation.

questions.question: Which statement about the “Workflows Path” is correct?

options.text: It consists of three lessons and is labeled as Beginner level.
options.explanation: The article lists the Workflows Path as having four lessons and being Intermediate.

options.text: It includes four lessons and is categorized as Intermediate.
options.explanation: The description for the Workflows Path states “4 Lessons” and “Intermediate”.

options.text: It is an Advanced path with five lessons focused on AI.
options.explanation: Five‑lesson paths are the Advanced Path, not the Workflows Path.

options.text: It is a Beginner path that focuses solely on AI fundamentals.
options.explanation: The Workflows Path is Intermediate and covers broader workflow creation, not just AI fundamentals.

questions.question: To become a Notion Certified Admin, what does the article say you must do?

options.text: Complete only the Advanced Path.
options.explanation: Certification is not tied to a single path; the article mentions a dedicated certification program.

options.text: Get certified to run Notion at scale, building smarter workspaces and implementing security best practices.
options.explanation: The article’s “Become a Notion Certified Admin” section describes exactly this requirement.

options.text: Attend a live webinar on Notion basics.
options.explanation: Webinars are supplemental; they are not listed as the certification requirement.

options.text: Purchase any paid Notion plan.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states certification is available for any plan type.

questions.question: Which learning format is NOT mentioned as part of Notion Academy’s offerings?

options.text: Live webinars.
options.explanation: Live webinars are listed under “Attend a live webinar.”

options.text: On‑demand sessions.
options.explanation: On‑demand sessions are mentioned alongside live webinars.

options.text: In‑person classroom training.
options.explanation: The article does not reference any in‑person classroom component.

options.text: Community events.
options.explanation: Community involvement is highlighted with “Join our community.”

questions.question: Which lesson is categorized as Beginner level and focuses on “Sharing & collaboration”?

options.text: Templates & marketplace.
options.explanation: That lesson is listed as Intermediate.

options.text: Building basics.
options.explanation: Building basics is a Beginner lesson but its focus is navigation and databases, not sharing.

options.text: Sharing & collaboration.
options.explanation: The article lists a Beginner lesson titled “Sharing & collaboration.”

options.text: Project management.
options.explanation: Project management is an Intermediate lesson.

questions.question: Which Notion Academy path is described as helping users “Start automating work, learn workspace architecture best practices, and harness Notion’s more complex capabilities?”

options.text: Essentials Path.
options.explanation: Essentials Path is for beginners learning basic skills.

options.text: Workflows Path.
options.explanation: Workflows Path focuses on creating connected systems, not specifically on advanced automation and architecture.

options.text: Advanced Path.
options.explanation: The article defines the Advanced Path as covering automation, workspace architecture, and complex capabilities.

options.text: Custom Agents Path.
options.explanation: Custom Agents is a single lesson, not a multi‑lesson path.

questions.question: Based on the article, which description best captures the functionality of “Custom Agents”?

options.text: AI‑powered chatbots that generate content for Notion pages.
options.explanation: The article does not mention content generation; it focuses on automation and triggers.

options.text: Tools that automate repeat work, can run on a schedule or respond to triggers, and integrate with the tools you already use.
options.explanation: This matches the article’s description of Custom Agents.

options.text: Pre‑made templates for project management.
options.explanation: Templates are a separate lesson; Custom Agents are about automation.

options.text: Community moderation tools for managing discussions.
options.explanation: Community moderation is not mentioned in the Custom Agents description.
